title: Chapter Sixty-Six: The Sect Lord Descends
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"Now, now you say the Sect Lord is coming?"

Won-chang's voice cracked on the last word. Beside him, Wei Ji Myo-hwa's hand had gone to her sword hilt, knuckles white. Even her cultivation -- built at Mount Zhongnan, sacred ground of the Dao Gate -- could not keep the shock from her face.

"Desolate Sect's lord. Personally," she said.

"It has been fifteen years since she stepped into the martial world," Cheong Myeong said slowly. "Since the destruction of the Dali Kingdom. A long time."

Fifteen years. In the murim, that was a full generation of rising talents. Long enough for young masters -- fat on spirit medicine and their own reputations -- to forget what an absolute power truly looked like.

Myo-hwa's fingers traced the length of her blade. "The divine technique that destroyed the Dali royal family. I saw it. With my own eyes."

She was staring at nothing. Remembering.

"I cannot imagine how fast her lightness technique might be," Cheong Myeong said, turning to Won-chang. "Tell the magistrate to prepare hospitality. Call the prefecture governor as well. She must be received with the courtesy due a monarch."

"Even the prefecture governor!"

Won-chang left at a near-run. The prefecture governor outranked the magistrate by three grades. He held authority no less than the Namgung family head. Even so, he would kneel before Desolate Sect's lord.

For she was a queen recognized by the emperor himself.

---

"The Sect Lord will be arriving. Prepare."

"...What did you say?"

Won-chang watched with grim satisfaction as the magistrate's face drained white. This was the same magistrate who had dragged his feet on every request to dispatch government troops. The same one who ate from the Namgung family's hand, who had turned a deaf ear when Yeon-shin was taken.

His lukewarm indifference vanished without a trace.

Won-chang stepped out of the workroom into the main hall.

A middle-aged swordsman in blue martial robes stood waiting. His gaze fixed on Won-chang like a blade settling into its groove.

The energy waves filling the hall were heavy. Layered. The man had not consciously raised his qi, yet the pressure was considerable.

*Azure Sky Boundless Sword.*

Won-chang recognized the technique immediately. The blue robes confirmed it. Namgung family.

"You Desolate Sect lot," the swordsman said. A neat jet-black beard framed a mouth set in contempt. "Are plotting something outrageous. Gathering warriors in South Zhili while ignoring our main family. With such discourtesy to local noble families, you are no different from evil sect rabble."

"Who are you?"

"I am Namgung Wi."

Won-chang was silent for a breath. He knew the name. Vice leader of the Azure Sky Sword Heaven Group. One of the top swordsmen in South Zhili. A master that even blue-ranked experts could not guarantee victory against.

*My neck could fly off if I am not careful.*

The Namgung family's collusion with local government ran deep. They had the power to bury a murder committed in the middle of a government office.

Won-chang opened his mouth.

"You filthy hypocrite." His voice was calm. The anger that had been accumulating for months flowed out steady and even, like water from a cracked dam. "Do you have the heart to speak of courtesy while knowing your family's baseless behavior? How did you memorize Confucius and Mencius and the Four Books and Three Classics, only to utter such words with a human face and a beast's heart? Do you feel no shame before your likely deceased parents?"

A gentle aura spread from his body. Warm. Unflinching.

Namgung Wi's reaction was delayed by a breath.

"What did you say?"

"You are the true evil sect warrior. You ignored the death of a rising talent who tried to behead evil cultists to protect common folk. Now the main sect gathers forces to strike that evil, and you speak of family face while turning a blind eye to your own--"

The internal energy rising from Namgung Wi's body grew heavier with every word.

Won-chang paid no heed.

"Your appearance is truly a sight to behold."

He finished with a clear expression. Refreshed.

Namgung Wi shook his head slowly.

"Did you think I would not draw my sword because this is a government office?"

*Woong.*

The blade began to cry. A majestic sound -- extraordinary, even for a renowned swordsman.

Won-chang was filling his throat with qi to call for Cheong Myeong when it happened.

*Thud.*

There was no cutting sound. No flash of steel. Just a gentle breeze, sudden and sweet, and Namgung Wi's head separated from his body. It fell to the floor like a leaf dropping from a branch.

So natural.

On the far side of the rolling head, the body of the renowned swordsman collapsed.

It did not feel real.

A breeze circled through the hall -- ceaseless, warm, like a heat haze given motion. Though the wind was colorless, it seemed as if faint green rippled outward in all directions.

Time in the government office froze.

"The Namgung family's crimes." A melodious voice rang from the rooftop. "Will be buried with the Lightning Flash."

Long robes the color of new grass fluttered against the sky.

Won-chang's head tilted up. His eyes widened -- tearing. He knelt. Proper bearing, back straight, but his body trembled with something that went far beyond relief.

She had come.

---

"Awake?"

A playful whisper, warm against his ear. Thick with inner power. He was used to it now.

The Seventh Apostle's breath.

Yeon-shin opened his eyes. His senses were sluggish -- true qi activation lagging half a beat behind intent. He could have forced it, could have lit up his entire body's energy sense by will alone. But the delay told him everything.

*Paralysis points sealed. Again.*

She was close. Her face filled half his vision. The expression was wrong -- deep affection mixed with something hungrier, a possessiveness that made his skin crawl.

He stared at her single red outer eye.

"What did you do?"

"I want to preserve your dazzling body. Preciously." She smiled, bright and guileless. "I drew a picture. So the child I see will remain in our sect forever."

She straightened and held up a large sheet of paper. The smell of ink and paint hit him -- stimulating, sharp. The drawing was not a portrait.

An anatomical diagram.

His entire body reproduced in meticulous detail. Eight extraordinary vessels. Blood paths. The three dantians. And one section drawn with terrifying precision: the upper dantian of his head, connected to the blood paths opened wide from his crown.

She had scanned his body with true qi while he slept. Observed his internal architecture with the casual thoroughness of a master butcher studying a carcass.

"Who knows?" The Seventh Apostle raised the corners of her mouth. "Perhaps our sect can create a child who can follow even a fraction of your talent."

"Strange divine arts," Yeon-shin said. "So the rumors are true. You use regenerative abilities for sinister medical experiments. No wonder righteous doctors fall behind."

"This will be a treasure."

"You use sect members as sacrifices. Fitting for evil sect mongrels."

Their words did not meet. A captor smiling. A prisoner calm. Misaligned from the start.

He closed his eyes.

The Demon Roaring Blood Technique's verses unfolded in his mind -- the secret manual the Blood Flame Sect Leader had given him. He had spent his waking hours classifying the effects. The brain-affecting verses were now obvious. He stripped them out.

*Done.*

After dismantling the madness, the rest was instantaneous. A new true qi operation path engraved itself in his mind like water finding its channel.

He could now neutralize the Seventh Apostle's qi that held his meridians sealed. He could break free whenever he chose.

And he had learned something else.

*The blood techniques' regenerative ability originates from true qi absorption. The martial arts are two-in-one. Even knowing them, they cannot be used openly outside.*

He was already thinking about escape. But first, there was work to do.

"Your martial arts," he said.

"Hm? Hm?"

"I am working on the Demon Roaring Blood Technique. Tailored just for you. Though it belongs to all Apostles."

"Ah!"

Her face lit with pure joy. "The other wretches have too much pride to learn it! You just need to look at me! The Sect Leader will focus on the Blood Summoning Technique!"

She pressed a folded package into his hands. Red. Heavy silk. A robe.

"Pure Blood Robe. Woven with our sect's sorcery and heavenly silkworm silk. Even ordinary swords cannot scratch it."

"Precious."

"Only Apostles can be granted them."

His Desolate Sect martial clothes had already been confiscated. He looked down at the package and let the silence stretch.

"Let us go for a walk," he said.

He needed to plant energy in structures throughout the compound. The formation he had studied yesterday -- the Two Flame Blood Spirit Formation -- had given him the key. He knew how to lock power into still objects so deeply that not even the Sect Leader could sense them.

*I need to see every railing, every wall, every stone step in this place.*

"You still have lingering attachment to Desolate Sect?" the Seventh Apostle asked, tilting her head.

He did not answer.

She cycled through expressions by herself -- anger, possessiveness, a flicker of something almost like regret.

*It is not for my sake. It never was.*

He opened the door wide.

A voice he had been hearing since midday grew louder. From the village center, a man stood on a raised platform before hundreds of red-haired sect members, arms spread wide.

"A new Grand Teacher has manifested in our sect! Your head will become equal to all Apostles! Therefore, you shall swing the sword of punishment against those vicious Beijing crowds and hypocritical ruffians!"

"The Third Apostle's son," the Seventh Apostle said. "Gao Zong-xue."

Yeon-shin studied him. From what the Seventh Apostle had said in the Sect Leader's hall, he knew the Third Apostle and his son were maneuvering for the small sect leader position. Political strife. Even among madmen.

Gao Zong-xue turned his head. Their eyes met.

The man's mouth curled upward. "There! He has shown his noble face to us!"

Hundreds of gazes fixed on Yeon-shin.

He shook out the package. The bright red cloth unfolded and snapped in the wind like a banner. Without a word, he layered the Pure Blood Robe over his body.

Silence.

*Woong.*

He began circulating the Demon Roaring Blood Technique -- the madness cut out, the verses clean. An aura that had never before come from the Blood Flame Sect.

Blood technique energy, but transparent. Calm where it should have been frenzied. The watching sect members sank to their knees. The sound of hundreds of knees hitting earth rolled through the village like distant thunder.

Black hair swayed at the shoulders of the red robe. Blood-colored fabric and raven hair.

At this moment, he looked no different from the Blood Flame Sect's orthodox lineage.

"You want to see the Grand Teacher's miracle?" he asked.

His crown pulsed. Once.

He ignored it.

---
